KPI Bluebook

Key Performance Indicators:

  • Ethylene Yield Percentage
  • Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio
  • Daily Feedstock Throughput (tons/day)
  • Specific Steam Consumption (ton/ton)
  • Energy Intensity Index (EII)
  • Cooling Water Delta-T (°C)
  • Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF)
  • Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE)
  • Pump Vibration Anomaly Rate
  • Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate (LTIFR)
  • SOx Emissions Concentration (kg/m³)
  • Flare Gas Recovery Rate
  • Polymer Melt Flow Index (MFI) Variance
  • On-Spec Production Percentage
  • Specific Catalyst Cost ($/ton)
  • Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio
KPI Bluebook
  • Pump Vibration Anomaly Rate
  • View page source

Pump Vibration Anomaly Rate

\[\text{}\]
Open in: VS Code PyCharm
Description
  • The Pump Vibration Anomaly Rate measures the percentage of vibration readings from pumps that exceed a predefined safe threshold
  • This KPI is crucial for identifying potential issues in pump operation before they lead to failures
Objective
  • The objective of monitoring the Pump Vibration Anomaly Rate is to enhance predictive maintenance strategies
  • allowing for timely interventions that can prevent equipment failures and reduce downtime
Formula

The formula for calculating the Pump Vibration Anomaly Rate involves determining the ratio of the number of vibration readings that exceed the threshold to the total number of readings, expressed as a percentage.

$$ \mathit{Vibration\,Threshold} = \frac{\mathit{5.0\,#\,Mm}}{\mathit{S}} $$
  • 5.0 # Mm: Value used in the calculation as defined by the code implementation.
  • S: Value used in the calculation as defined by the code implementation.
Used in KPI(s)
  • This KPI is used in predictive maintenance and reliability assessments to ensure the operational integrity of pumps within the facility
Input Measure
  • Vibration readings from pumps
  • which are collected over a specified period
Unit of Measure ratio (dimensionless)
Reporting Source The data for this KPI is sourced from vibration sensors installed on the pumps, which continuously monitor and log vibration levels.
Comments A high anomaly rate may indicate underlying issues such as misalignment, wear, or other mechanical problems that require immediate attention to avoid costly repairs and operational disruptions.

Code Context

 1:emphasize-lines: 7
 2
 3
 4def calculate_pump_vibration_anomaly_rate(vibration_readings: list) -> float:
 5 """Calculates the percentage of vibration readings that exceed a safe threshold."""
 6 if not vibration_readings:
 7     return 0.0
 8 vibration_threshold = 5.0  # mm/s
 9 anomalies = [r for r in vibration_readings if r > vibration_threshold]
10 # KPI: Pump Vibration Anomaly Rate
11 # A predictive maintenance KPI used to anticipate pump failures and plan proactive maintenance.
12 anomaly_rate = (len(anomalies) / len(vibration_readings)) * 100
13 return anomaly_rate
